raw_consumer_statement:
A XXXX or XXXX before XX/XX/year> ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ), I called XXXX XXXX in XXXX XXXX to make a reservation for XXXX people on XXXX XXXX XXXX. The hostess told me that she wasn't taking normal reservations because they were having a special XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. I told her I wanted to make reservations for the XXXX. She asked me for my credit card information, which I thought she was using just to hold the reservation, so I gave it to her. The reservation was for XXXX people. I never even received a receipt. We showed up to the restaurant, but only XXXX of us actually ate a XXXX. XXXX person in our party wasn't able to show up, and my XXXX little brothers wanted to eat somewhere else, so they didn't eat at XXXX XXXX. After XXXX of us finished eating, I talked to the hostess about our bill. I told her we should only be charged for XXXXXXXX XXXX since only XXXX people ate. She told me that the XXXX would call me the next day to figure it out. The XXXX never called me. I tried calling and emailing the restaurant, but they kept ignoring me. First of all, I didn't even want this purchase to be on this credit card. I wanted it to be charged to a different card, but the merchant refused/ignored me. Also, I was charged for XXXX meals when we only ate XXXX meals. They have cameras in their restaurant, so they have proof that only XXXX of us ate. 

Because the XXXX XXXX to talk to me to resolve the issue, I started a dispute with the credit card company ( Citi ). However, Citi took the merchant 's side, even though it's a law that you can't charge people for food in a restaurant if they never ate it. It is impossible to get into contact with anyone from the disputes department at Citi. Their automated system does not allow their customer to reach an agent there. 

Additionally, Citi claims that the merchant responded to this dispute. However, I looked at the letters on my online account, and no merchant response is there. I have attached the letters to this complaint.
